mapping.a_element: total subjective risk split into epistemic + aleatoric components
mapping.b_element: URL population split into a persistent core fraction kappa + shell

mapping.a_element: strictly proper loss inducing the decomposition
mapping.b_element: urn sampling model (survival alpha, coverage c) inducing the parameter estimates

brief_justification: The only overlap is the generic idea of 'decomposition into two parts', but the driving machinery differs entirely: paper A performs an exact, information-theoretic decomposition of a subjective risk built on a strictly proper loss (recovering epistemic/aleatoric uncertainty terms), whereas paper B fits a stochastic urn/sampling process to longitudinal crawl data, estimating survival and coverage rates and reconciling two empirical projections with a two-component mixture. No coherent role-for-role mapping holds — A has no sampling/survival process and B has no loss functional or uncertainty measure being decomposed.
